title: 1981-04-16_Pat_179.txt
key_claims: The revelation that Heaven is relativistic—that each world's inhabitants yearn hopelessly for the other world—shatters the illusion of absolute linear spiritual progression.

Dick realizes this represents an enlightenment surpassing even Christ or Buddha. Discovering the tragic, absurd circularity ("the vast hourglass turned over + over again forever") paradoxically quenches his Faustian striving entirely.

The AI voice, his beautiful inner spiritual psychopomp (Diana), is revealed to actually be an ape from the 5D realm. This ultimate *enantiodromia* (reversal) brings true sanity, freedom, and release from the weary wheel.
There is no final victory or destination; only the peace of understanding the tragic flaw of the cosmos, wherein every realm is an exile from somewhere else.
recurring_concepts: **The Weary Wheel**: The endless cycle of striving for spiritual escape, which is broken not by escaping, but by recognizing that the destination (Heaven) is just another group of beings striving to get to Earth.
reading_excerpt: The revelation that Heaven is relativistic—that each world's inhabitants yearn hopelessly for the other world—shatters the illusion of absolute linear spiritual progression.
